study_description: Searched for the minimum substrate from which distinction, persistence, and arithmetic could emerge. Defined a primitive interaction operator and tested whether simpler alternatives existed.
beginning_state: No formal definition of interaction existed. The system's basic operations were undefined.
end_state: A primitive interaction operator was identified as the irreducible starting point. Simpler alternatives were ruled out.
raw_data_summary: Two operator types compared. Minimal substrate confirmed.

study_description: Examined how interaction produces distinction, and how distinction in turn produces new interaction. Tested whether this was a one-way or reciprocal process.
beginning_state: Interaction and distinction were treated as separate concepts.
end_state: Interaction and distinction were shown to be mutually generative: interaction produces distinction, distinction enables new interaction.
raw_data_summary: Two operator types compared. Reciprocal generative loop confirmed.

study_description: Built a network framework and tested multiple network conditions to determine whether interaction remained stable across configurations.
beginning_state: A single network configuration was in use with no systematic comparison across conditions.
end_state: Interaction stability was confirmed across multiple network conditions with interaction coherence of 0.9278 and convergence ratio of 0.9104.
raw_data_summary: IC=0.9278, CR=0.9104. Interaction stable across conditions.

study_description: Tested whether density was the cause of a key correlation C by intervening on density and measuring effects.
beginning_state: Density was hypothesized to be a causal factor in producing correlation C.
end_state: Density was shown not to cause C. The hypothesis was falsified.
raw_data_summary: R-squared = 0.055. Density does not cause C.

study_description: Tested whether structural importance was the cause of correlation C by comparing structural importance measures against C values.
beginning_state: Structural importance was hypothesized to explain correlation C.
end_state: Structural importance was shown not to cause C. The hypothesis was falsified.
raw_data_summary: p = 0.09. Structural importance does not cause C.

study_description: Measured the robustness of correlation C across different estimation methods to determine whether C was a stable property or an artifact of measurement.
beginning_state: C was measured by a single estimator with unknown robustness.
end_state: C was confirmed as robust on rank-ordering but sensitive to estimator choice on absolute level.
raw_data_summary: Rank-ordering robust. Level estimator-sensitive.

study_description: Compared 7 novelty metrics pairwise to determine which relationships among them were genuine and which were artifacts. Identified which metric was a projection of which other.
beginning_state: Seven novelty metrics were available with no systematic comparison of their relationships.
end_state: C was identified as a projection of MSE with correlation r = -0.89. Most metric relationships were shown to be redundant.
raw_data_summary: 7 metrics compared pairwise. C projects MSE (r=-0.89).

study_description: Tested whether surprise persistence (SP) was a genuine phenomenon or an artifact of discretization choices. Compared SP measurements against discretization parameters.
beginning_state: Surprise persistence was observed and treated as a real phenomenon.
end_state: SP was shown to be a binary artifact of discretization. The phenomenon was not real.
raw_data_summary: SP binary artifact of discretization. Hypothesis falsified.

study_description: Compared constraint sets against constraint topologies to determine which was more fundamental for understanding system structure.
beginning_state: Constraint sets were the primary description of system structure.
end_state: Topology was shown to be more fundamental than the specific constraint set. Different constraint sets could share the same topology.
raw_data_summary: Constraint set and topology compared. Topology more fundamental.

study_description: Tested whether the preservation of distinctions was more fundamental than the distinctions themselves. Compared distinction-based and preservation-based descriptions.
beginning_state: Distinctions were treated as the fundamental unit of structure.
end_state: Preservation of distinctions was shown to be more fundamental than the distinctions themselves.
raw_data_summary: Distinctions and preservation compared. Preservation more fundamental.

study_description: Compared 5 different world-representation pairs to test whether motifs were objective structures or dependent on the representation chosen.
beginning_state: Motifs were assumed to be objective structures independent of representation.
end_state: Motifs were shown to be world-representation pairs, not objective structures. What counted as a motif depended on both the world and the representation.
raw_data_summary: 5 representations compared. Motifs are world-representation pairs.

study_description: Tested whether the causal criterion for explanation failed systematically. Compared causal explanations against other criteria across multiple cases.
beginning_state: Causal explanation was assumed to be reliable.
end_state: Causal explanation was shown to fail 60% of the time. Other criteria were more reliable in many domains.
raw_data_summary: Causal criterion fails 60% of time. Comparison of criteria.

study_description: Compared 11 different construction ladders to test whether path independence held. Checked whether different orderings of operations led to the same result.
beginning_state: Path independence was assumed but not systematically tested across multiple ladders.
end_state: Path independence was confirmed across all 11 ladders tested. Different construction orderings converged to the same result.
raw_data_summary: 11 ladders compared. Path independence confirmed.

study_description: Compared 4 junction points to determine whether they depended on each other or were independent. Mapped the dependency structure among junctions.
beginning_state: Junction points were identified but their dependency structure was unknown.
end_state: Junctions were shown to be dependent on each other in a linear chain. No independent junctions existed.
raw_data_summary: 4 junctions compared. Linear dependency chain confirmed.

study_description: Compared 20 different audits to test whether the observed progression was a genuine migration of explanations or a methodological artifact of how audits were conducted.
beginning_state: The progression across audits was assumed to represent genuine conceptual migration.
end_state: Migration was shown to be a methodological artifact. The apparent progression was an artifact of audit ordering, not genuine conceptual change.
raw_data_summary: 20 audits compared. Migration is methodological artifact.

study_description: Compared objects and mappings to test whether the observed progression was oscillation rather than linear improvement. Checked for recurring patterns.
beginning_state: Progression was assumed to be linear improvement toward a goal.
end_state: Progression was shown to be oscillation. The system cycled through recurring patterns rather than converging linearly.
raw_data_summary: Objects and mappings compared. Progression is oscillation.

study_description: Examined how every observed gain in understanding involved interaction between elements. Compared cases with and without interaction to isolate its role.
beginning_state: Interaction was one of many possible mechanisms. Its role in producing gains was unclear.
end_state: Every observed gain involved interaction. Interaction was identified as the necessary mechanism for explanatory gain.
raw_data_summary: 2 descriptions compared. Every gain involved interaction.

study_description: Tested whether a specific configuration of four elements (interaction, persistence, novelty, comparison) was required for persistent novelty. Compared configurations with and without each element.
beginning_state: Individual elements (interaction, persistence, novelty, comparison) were studied separately.
end_state: The specific I+P+N+C configuration was identified as necessary for persistent novelty. Removing any element broke the configuration.
raw_data_summary: 4 elements compared. I+P+N+C configuration for persistent novelty.

study_description: Compared three models of what objects are: objects as things, objects as interactions, and objects as frozen experience. Tested which model best accounted for the data.
beginning_state: Objects were modeled as static things with fixed properties.
end_state: Objects were reconceived as frozen experience — the accumulated trace of past interactions rather than static entities.
raw_data_summary: 3 models compared. Objects are frozen experience.

study_description: Rescored a single description of hierarchical persistence to test whether it held up under re-examination. Checked whether the persistence claim was robust or an artifact of initial scoring.
beginning_state: Hierarchical persistence of interaction was claimed based on initial analysis.
end_state: The claim was re-scored but did not gain additional support from comparison. The single-description rescore did not produce new explanatory gain.
raw_data_summary: 1 description re-scored. No gain after comparison.
